name: RAG on living data
description: Automated workflow: RAG on living data. This workflow integrates 18 different services: notionTrigger, stickyNote, textSplitterTokenSplitter, vectorStoreSupabase, chainRetrievalQa. It contains 36 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
shortDescription: RAG on living data workflow containing 35 nodes.

originalWorkflowJson.description: Automated workflow: RAG on living data. This workflow integrates 18 different services: notionTrigger, stickyNote, textSplitterTokenSplitter, vectorStoreSupabase, chainRetrievalQa. It contains 36 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
originalWorkflowJson.notes: Excellent quality workflow: RAG on living data. This workflow has been optimized for production use with comprehensive error handling, security, and documentation.

parameters.content:
## Switch trigger (optional)
If you are on the cloud plan, consider switching to the Notion Trigger Node instead, to save on executions.

## Adjust chunk size and overlap
For more accurate search results, increase the overlap. For the *text-embedding-ada-002* model the chunk size plus overlap must not exceed 8191
This placeholder serves as a reference point so it is easier to swap the data source with a different service
All chunks of a previous version of the document are being deleted by filtering the meta data by the given ID
Reduce the active streams/items to just 1 to prevent the following nodes from double-processing
Retrieve all page contents/blocks
Reduce the active streams/items to just 1 to prevent the following nodes from double-processing
Embed item and store in Vector Store. Depending on the length the content is being split up into multiple chunks/embeds
Store additional meta data with each embed, especially the Notion ID, which can be later used to find all belonging entries of one page, even if they got split into multiple embeds.
Simple chat bot to ask specific questions while having access to the context of the Notion Knowledge Base which was stored in the Vector Store
Model used for both creating and reading embeddings
Process each page/document separately.
Using a manual approach for polling data from Notion for more accuracy.

## Select Database
Choose the database which represents your Knowledge Base
Combine all contents to a single text formatted into one line which can be easily stored as an embed

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Open the scheduler node "Schedule Trigger".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Verify the settings of the trigger node "Notion Trigger".
Open the "Embeddings OpenAI" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Token Splitter" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
